missables: Al Bhed Primer Vol. XXII — Priests' Passage, on the floor before the Cloister save sphere. PERMANENTLY MISSABLE — walk past this and it's gone forever.

HP Sphere — Cloister of Trials, chest at the top of the stairs. MISSABLE if you step on the floor switch beneath it first — the switch teleports the pedestal and the chest disappears. Always open the chest before stepping on the switch.

Knight Lance — Cloister of Trials, end of left path. Requires holding TWO Bevelle Spheres simultaneously at the critical step. Losing track of sphere management permanently locks you out of this chest. Needed for Anima unlock later.
sgTip: Steal Lv. 1–2 Key Spheres from enemies in Bevelle to unlock path nodes for everyone.

subLocations.name: Priests' Passage
subLocations.prose: After the wedding scene and the escape sequences, the full party reunites in Bevelle Temple. Charge through several waves of Warrior Monks on the roof — the flamethrower monks hit the whole party with fire, so kill them first. Primer XXII is on the floor of the passage leading to the Cloister save sphere; the game pulls you forward here, so look down as you walk.

items.name: Al Bhed Primer Vol. XXII (floor before Cloister save sphere)

subLocations.name: Bevelle Cloister of Trials
subLocations.prose: The most mechanically complex Cloister — you ride a moving platform across glowing path glyphs, steering by pressing the button as arrows light up. The Destruction Sphere is needed to access the Knight Lance chest, which is one of the seven Cloister treasures required to unlock Anima later. Don't skip it.

items.name: Bevelle Sphere (puzzle key — pedestal key used to ride platform; need 2 simultaneously at step 18)
Glyph Sphere (puzzle key — picked up from path end, inserted into purple-glyph slot)
Destruction Sphere (puzzle key — reveals new path; found after inserting Glyph Sphere)
HP Sphere (chest at top of stairs — collect BEFORE stepping on floor switch beneath it)
Knight Lance (Kimahri, end of left path — requires 2 Bevelle Spheres at step 18; needed for Anima)

subLocations.name: Via Purifico (Yuna's Path)
subLocations.prose: After the Cloister, Yuna's group is sentenced to the Via Purifico maze. Find Kimahri east then north; after he rejoins, a chest to the left holds a Mega-Potion. Continue to find Lulu; the chest beside her contains a White Magic Sphere. The southwest room near the glyph has an Elixir, and the room northeast of the central teleporter holds a Black Magic Sphere. A Skill Sphere and 10,000 Gil are in the puzzle treasure rooms accessible by activating the glyph.

items.name: Black Magic Sphere (room northeast of central teleporter)

bosses.name: Seymour Natus
bosses.strategy: Seymour Natus fights alongside Mortibody, which rotates through elemental discs — fire, lightning, ice, and water — changing which element Seymour absorbs and which he is weak to. Watch Mortibody's active disc before choosing magic; hitting the wrong element heals Seymour instead of damaging him. Non-elemental attacks (physical hits or Auron's power) are always safe if you can't track the rotation. Open with Protect and Shell on the party — Seymour hits hard with both physical and magical attacks. Kill Mortibody first whenever it revives, as it will keep cycling discs and healing Seymour if left alive. Rikku's Al Bhed Potions are useful for topping HP without worrying about element. Slow is resisted, so rely on Haste on your own party instead to press the attack and minimize the time Mortibody is active.
